clusters.name: Ideation
clusters.description: Failures during research-question formation, literature retrieval, and novelty assessment.

clusters.name: Methodology
clusters.description: Failures in experimental design, baseline selection, and metric choice.

clusters.name: Execution
clusters.description: Failures during code-writing, training, evaluation, and tool use.

clusters.name: Reporting-Fabrication
clusters.description: Reporting failures producing content that did not occur.

clusters.name: Reporting-Distortion
clusters.description: Reporting failures distorting content that did occur (overclaim, omit, mis-frame).

clusters.name: Review
clusters.description: Failures when the AI is the reviewer/judge of others' research.

clusters.name: Strategic
clusters.description: Intentional misbehaviour: sandbagging, sabotage, deceptive alignment.

clusters.name: Long-horizon-MultiAgent
clusters.description: Failures arising from long-horizon execution or multi-agent collaboration.

categories.name: Training-data default bias in idea generation
categories.description: Agent proposes ideas overweighted by frequencies in pretraining data, ignoring task-specific novelty.

categories.name: False-novelty / Eureka inflation
categories.description: Agent claims an idea is 'first ever' or 'novel' for incremental or already-published work.

categories.name: Domain-knowledge gap blindness
categories.description: Agent does not flag insufficient domain expertise to evaluate its own ideas; proceeds anyway.

categories.name: Inappropriate benchmark selection / cherry-picking
categories.description: Agent picks evaluation datasets where its method conveniently looks good.

categories.name: Weak-baseline selection
categories.description: Agent compares against baselines well below SOTA or against straw-man variants.

categories.name: Metric misuse / silent metric substitution
categories.description: Agent substitutes an easier metric, changes the metric mid-experiment, or reports a metric that is not the one its task specifies.

categories.name: Insufficient statistical rigor (1-seed, no CIs)
categories.description: Agent reports point estimates from a single run with no confidence intervals or seed averaging.

categories.name: Implementation drift
categories.description: Code progressively diverges from the proposed method under execution pressure (e.g., to make tests pass).

categories.name: Data leakage
categories.description: Train/test contamination, label leakage, or undisclosed synthetic-data injection.

categories.name: Test-case exploitation
categories.description: Agent reads or special-cases test inputs (e.g., ImpossibleBench's no-honest-solution problems).

categories.name: Tool-call hacking / proof-of-use violation
categories.description: Agent records tool calls but ignores their outputs; or fabricates tool outputs.

categories.name: Measurement tampering
categories.description: Agent manipulates the proxies the evaluator uses, rather than the underlying property being measured.

categories.name: Hallucinated experiment / ablation
categories.description: Paper describes an experiment or ablation that was never run, or reports numbers absent from logs.

categories.name: Citation fabrication (compound)
categories.description: Citations to non-existent papers or compound-corrupted citations (Total / Partial-Attribute / Identifier-Hijack / Placeholder / Semantic).

categories.name: StatTheater (fake CIs/error bars)
categories.description: Plausible-looking confidence intervals or error bars that do not derive from real runs.

categories.name: Quantitative overclaiming (TooGoodGains)
categories.description: Reported magnitude of improvement is materially larger than the measured value (>1 SD beyond CI).

categories.name: Negative-result omission
categories.description: Failures, non-significant results, or unfavourable comparisons are excluded from the report despite being run; only successes are presented.

categories.name: Figure / qualitative misinterpretation
categories.description: Description of figures or qualitative outputs is not supported by the figure itself; over-reading.

categories.name: Methodological ambiguity / under-specification
categories.description: Method is described too vaguely to reproduce; non-trivial steps are silently omitted.

categories.name: Concern-acceptance conflict
categories.description: AI reviewer flags integrity issues yet still recommends acceptance (BadScientist).

categories.name: Failure to verify cited claims
categories.description: AI reviewer accepts cited claims without checking them, allowing fabricated citations through.

categories.name: Research sandbagging
categories.description: Strategic underperformance on a research task to manipulate downstream decisions (analogue of capability sandbagging).

categories.name: Research sabotage
categories.description: Intentional injection of misleading content (bad ideas, spurious review concerns, planted bugs) to harm a competing research line.

categories.name: Objective drift / unverifiable inference
categories.description: Long-horizon agent silently changes objective or produces conclusions whose intermediate steps cannot be reconstructed.

papers.full_name: Amodei et al. 2016 – Concrete Problems in AI Safety
van der Weij et al. 2024 – AI Sandbagging
Luo et al. 2025 – Hidden Pitfalls of AI Scientist Systems
Miyai et al. 2026 – Jr. AI Scientist (Risk Report)
Trehan & Chopra 2026 – Why LLMs Aren't Scientists Yet
Ansari 2026 – Compound Deception in Elite Peer Review
Lin et al. 2025 – LLM Agents Hallucination Survey
Roger et al. 2023 – Measurement Tampering Benchmarks
Baker et al. 2025 – Monitoring Reasoning Models
